name: react-ui-patterns
description: Modern React UI patterns for loading states, error handling, and data fetching. Use when building UI components, handling async data, or managing UI states.
capabilities: Implement loading states that avoid UI flash when cached data exists
Handle errors gracefully with user-facing error messages and retry options
Create empty states for lists and collections with contextual messaging
Manage button states during form submissions to prevent duplicate actions
Apply optimistic updates for better perceived performance
Structure error handling hierarchy from inline to full-page errors
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 33 potential issues (external_commands, weak_crypto, system_reconnaissance), but manual review confirms all are false positives. The backticks detected are TypeScript template literals in code examples, not shell commands. No cryptographic code or system reconnaissance tools exist. This is a pure documentation skill containing only React UI pattern examples with no executable code, network calls, or file system access.
valueStatement: Building React components with proper loading states, error handling, and user feedback is challenging and often results in confusing UX. This skill provides battle-tested UI patterns for handling async operations, showing appropriate feedback, and creating smooth user experiences.
limitations: Does not include actual component implementations - only patterns and examples
Does not provide runtime code or utilities - documentation only
Does not integrate with specific state management libraries

useCases.title: Building data-driven React components
useCases.description: When creating components that fetch data from APIs, apply proper loading, error, and empty states following established patterns.

useCases.targetUser: Full-stack developers working with React forms
useCases.title: Handling form submissions
useCases.description: When implementing forms with async submissions, ensure buttons are disabled during submission and show appropriate loading feedback.

useCases.title: Improving user experience
useCases.description: When refactoring existing React code to eliminate confusing loading flashes and hidden errors.

promptTemplates.title: Basic loading state
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement a loading state for a React component that fetches data. I want to avoid showing a spinner when cached data already exists.

promptTemplates.title: Error handling pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an error state component for React that shows the error message and includes a retry button. How should I integrate this with Apollo or React Query?
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding error handling to data fetching

promptTemplates.title: Form submission with feedback
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a form submission pattern in React that disables the submit button during the mutation, shows a loading spinner, and displays success or error toasts.

promptTemplates.title: Complete data component
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a complete React component that handles all UI states: loading (with skeleton), error (with retry), empty (with call to action), and success (with data display).

bestPractices: Never show stale UI - loading spinners should only appear when there's no data to display
Always surface errors to users - never swallow errors silently without feedback
Disable buttons during async operations to prevent duplicate submissions
antiPatterns: Showing loading spinner whenever loading is true, regardless of whether cached data exists
Catching errors and only logging to console without showing user feedback
Allowing users to click submit multiple times during form submission

faq.question: What is the golden rule for loading states?
faq.answer: Show loading indicator ONLY when there's no data to display. This prevents UI flashes when refetching cached data.

faq.question: When should I use skeleton loaders vs spinners?
faq.answer: Use skeletons for known content shapes like lists and cards. Use spinners for unknown content shapes, modals, and button submissions.

faq.question: How do I handle errors in React?
faq.answer: Always show errors to users. Use onError handlers in mutations to display toasts or error states. Never silently catch errors.

faq.question: Why do I need empty states?
faq.answer: Every list and collection needs an empty state to guide users. Empty states should be contextual and provide actions when possible.

faq.question: How do I prevent double form submissions?
faq.answer: Disable the submit button during submission and show a loading indicator. Also validate form before enabling the button.

faq.question: What is optimistic UI updates?
faq.answer: Optimistic updates make the UI feel instant by immediately showing the expected result before the server confirms it. Roll back if the operation fails.
